description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily white, featuring blue accents on the engine nacelles, tail fin, and wingtips. The aircraft has two prominent underwing turbofan engines and a pair of long, swept-back wings with upward-angled winglets at the tips. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer with a blue and white star pattern, and a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible along the side for passengers. The branding 'Hi Fly' is displayed on the forward fuselage, indicating a commercial airline livery. The overall design is modern, with smooth, glossy surfaces and aerodynamic features typical of contemporary wide-body jets.
description: A modern, white commercial passenger airplane with blue accents, swept-back wings with winglets, and two underwing turbofan engines. The fuselage features 'Hi Fly' branding and a blue tail fin with stars.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, both engines under the wings, and the full wingspan with upward-angled winglets.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the full length of the fuselage, 'Hi Fly' branding, passenger windows, and the blue tail fin with stars.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the backs of the engines and wings.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the opposite side of the fuselage, engines, and tail fin details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, long swept-back wings with upward-angled winglets and slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear (not visible but implied by commercial jet design)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
